"You did seem happy." "And I was, but Percy's intense. I can tell what he wants and it's more than I'm willing to give." "Which is?" Padma asked. "Everything." "Did youÑ" "Talk to him? Don't be absurd?" And with that, Pansy got up and walked into the kitchen, effectively ending that portion of the conversation. They all stared at each other, knowing better than to revive it upon her return. When she came back with straight vodka, it was an even clearer sign to move on. And Ginny took it. "Hermione, I saw you and Malfoy walking around the joke shop together last week. You both seemedÉ" "Let me guess your next word." Hermione rolled her eyes. "Close. Ugh, not this again." "I'm just saying. Why notÑ" "Would you like those reasons listed chronologically, alphabetically"Ñshe hiccuppedÑ"or in order of importance?" Pansy scowled. "SometimesÑ" "Hermione has a point." Cho's defence caught everyone by surprise. "Malfoy's like a brick wall." "And you know this how?" Pansy narrowed her eyes. "I know he's closed off." "You don't know fuck all about Draco." Pansy looked ready to throw her drink and it was a wonder she didn't. "Don't sit here and talk about him like you do." "You're right, but he appears coldÑ" "You don't even know him well enough to say that." Hermione's words caught her by surprise. "No one has the right to put another person in a box, especially when they don't know them." "I know what I've seen, what I've heard, andÑ" "Can you justÉ not talk about him around me, especially sinceÑ" Hermione stopped herself from saying more than intended. The alcohol had certainly loosened her lips. "Since I've gotten to know him better." Cho looked confused. "But you just said you weren'tÑ" "I'm just not going to let you judge him when he's not here to defend himself. If you don't want people to make assumptions about you, you shouldn't do it to him." "Hermione's right," Padma said. "I know what you're dealing with but Malfoy doesn't deserve the judgment." "Stop talking about him like he's someÉ someÉ bloody exotic fish!" Hermione's mood was as hot as the room felt. "Y-you see his world. It looks perfect. He looks exactly like he should." She rubbed her head to clear the fog. "Before judging, look at his tank. I guarantee you'll see the bits that aren't right. He doesn't belong there. HeÉ shite. Draco's not a fish." For several moments, no one said anything. "That'sÉ deep." Parvati looked astonished while Cho glanced down at her hands. "It sounds like you've thought about it a lot." Ginny looked over at Pansy and found her similarly shocked. She had thought about it. Often. Without defending herself, Hermione got up and stumbled to the loo upstairs. When she returned, the music was back on, and everyone appeared to be enjoying themselves, but Pansy was gone. Ginny pointed in the direction of the conservatory before Hermione even asked. But Pansy wasn't there. Instead, she'd gone outside and was sitting on the swing, drinking Firewhisky straight from the bottle. Hermione joined her, not knowing what to say to someone so clearly in pain yet too stubborn to admit it. She rested a hand over Pansy's and waited in silence. Blinking at the orbs of light floating over the garden, the world began to swim. Pansy's fist balled up under her palm. "This wasn't supposed to happen." Pansy's words floated on the breeze, and when Hermione looked over, she noted a single tear rolling down her friend's cheek. "It's awful. I hate this." "What?" "Love." August 28, 2011 The weather was uncommonly nice. Despite the clouds in the sky that only allowed the sun to peek through every so often it wasn't going to rain. With flowers in bloom and warm breezes, it was calming to be outside rather than experience it from the comfort of her conservatory. Hermione let her chickens free, watching as they snapped up insects, beetles, and grubs in two particularly problematic beds. She left them to it, confined there by magicÑnot that they noticed. They were having plenty of fun. It wouldn't be enough to eliminate the problem, but it would help, and the chickens deserved a little freedom while she worked to clear and fertilise a different bed in preparation for winter planting. To make a real difference in her garden, she would need at least ten more chickens. The idea alone was daunting. Still, Hermione turned it over in her head as she snuck glances at her busy workers while they pecked at the dirt. It was hard to imagine having the capacity for new chickens. Neville would have to expand the coop, which would also require different warding. Time. Patience. Energy. All of those things were in short supply. But there was a quiet part of Hermione's mind that fawned over the idea of Scorpius holding a fluffy chick in his small hands. Naming it. Petting it. Caring for it. Showing it to Albus, eyes sparkling with excitement and pride. Something all his own. Something he could watch grow and change. An image Hermione couldn't quickly forget. She tabled the idea for later consideration. It was easy to notice when they were full because they started to go after the plants, pecking the leaves just because they couldÑchaotic little troublemakersÑso she corralled them all back into their coop to play until they got tired. Hermione continued clearing the flowerbed, testing the quality of the soil, and trying to figure out what sort of fertilising it would need. She then turned to watering the roses, absently making sure to clean the blooms of dirt. They looked better for it. Taking the harvested fruits and vegetables into the house, she made herself lunch on a break before she finished clearing out the second bed. Nothing heavy. Just a sandwich that she ate out in the pasture, stretched out on a blanket with a book she'd been meaning to read. After finishing her meal, she planned to waste the hour away with her book, but the atmosphere decided otherwise. The warm breeze lulled her senses andÉ Hermione woke up dazed. Laying on her side with her head resting on her arm, she noticed the book beside her. It was hard to tell how much time had passed until she glanced at her watch. More than an hour. After sitting up and stretching, she rolled her shoulders and turned her head from side to side to ease away the stiffness that came with sleep. She was about to pack up when she felt the tingle of her wards. She had a guest. And it wasn't one Hermione had expected. Narcissa. Her patient had finally been released from the hospital that morning. She was accompanied by Scorpius, which Hermione learned when she found them sitting together on the sofa in her living room. Narcissa looked slightly off; it piqued her curiosity because moments like that had increased since her incident. Scorpius waved, and Hermione responded in kind. But then he signed, and she dropped everything. Help. Wrong. "Narcissa?" The witch turned slowly to the sound of Hermione's voice. "How are you doing?" "Just fine." She stood up. "Be a dear and watch Draco for me." She sauntered off in the direction of the conservatory, leaving her and Scorpius exchanging looks. It took a few questions, signed responses, and headshakes to learn that her behaviour had changed as soon as she sat down. Scorpius took Hermione's offered hand and she led the way to the conservatory where Narcissa was not. Hermione nearly dropped his hand in panic, but tried to remain calm, quickly spotting Narcissa walking in the pasture. This was a dilemma. She didn't want to leave Scorpius alone, but she didn't know if she should bring him with her to chase after his grandmother either. She thought about calling for Draco, but remembered he was at a restoration meeting. She Floo called Andromeda, but she needed a few minutes to finish what she was doing before she was able to leave. Which left Catherine, who arrived not a minute after the green flames ceased. "Everything okay? Narcissa said she was coming here and Scorpius wanted to see you." "I need you to watch him." Hermione summoned a book of Al's, propped Scorpius on the swing, and gestured for Catherine to join him. "I'll be right back." Scorpius nodded, his face serious as he turned the first page. Narcissa hadn't gone far. It was probably the most casual Hermione had ever seen her, sitting at the centre of her blanket out in the pasture. Legs folded, gaze somewhere off in the distance, she lookedÉ helpless. When Hermione sat down, there was sadness in the very breath Narcissa exhaled. "I don't remember coming here." "You're safe." Hermione touched her shoulder and watched as the witch's eyes lifted to the blue skies. "Scorpius is safe, too. Everything is fine." "No. Everything is not fine." Narcissa's voice was barely louder than the rustle of the trees. "It's going to keep happening. I'm going to keep leaving and leaving until I don't come back one day. I'm going to keep forgetting and waking up in places not knowing how I got there. There is no end to this beyondÉ" For once, Hermione didn't know what to say. The cold truth was yes, she was right, but it didn't seem appropriate to respond with logic. Only emotion. But Narcissa beat her to it by resting her head on her shoulder in a move that shocked Hermione still. They simplyÉ sat. Just like that. Occasionally Hermione shot squinting glances over her shoulder to where she could barely make out Scorpius obediently looking down at the book in his lap. They sat. Hermione's arm curled around her, holding her in place, tethering her to reality. They sat. Listening to the sounds of birds, of nature, of life itself, the seconds rolled by. They sat until Narcissa finally spoke. "It's Lucius' birthday." She fiddled with the ring around her neck. "I miss him, but on hard days like today, I miss him the most." "What do you miss most about him?" While Hermione didn't know Lucius Malfoy outside of what she'd experienced and read about, he had been an important part of Narcissa's lifeÉ and her memories. Memories that she was losing. It probably felt like losing him all over again. More than once. Every incident. Every day. "I miss his voice. His smile. How he smelled and laughed. His presence." Narcissa fell silent. "Silly, I know." "No, it's not. It's how you feel and no one can tell you how to grieve." "On days like thisÉ" She lowered her head and took a deep breath. "I think of Scorpius and understand why you have been fighting so hard for him. I think of Draco and understand why he has been so angry at me." "Your son carries a lot of weight on his shoulders." "I have done him no favours." Narcissa never stopped touching the ring around her neck. "On some level, I have known all along. I have just been too stubborn to see." "Now that you understand better, you can do better." "Draco has beenÉ more amenable since my accident. He speaks to me now, and not just to be polite. He's trying. As am I." Hermione looked over her shoulder as she listened, feeling the wards tingle with a new arrival. Andromeda. Scorpius remained on the swing but he was no longer looking down, just straight ahead. "Do you want to come back into the house?" "No." Narcissa's voice was still distant. "I would like to sit here and remember while I can." Hermione let her do just that, swapping places with Andromeda, who sat next to her sister and held her. The contrast between them, right down to their hair colours, was startling against the green of the pasture surrounding them. Scorpius didn't ease off the swing until she was standing directly in front of him and nodding for Catherine to return to the house. He peered out in the pasture where his grandmother sat with Andromeda before turning worried eyes to her. "She's sad, but she's okay." It was the best explanation Hermione could give. Scorpius placed the book on the swing and led the way to the greenhouse, waiting patiently by the door for her to open it. Hermione followed the little boy who had a specific destination in mind. The tangerine tree. It was full of ripened fruit and Hermione summoned a few after a long look. Not knowing what he had in mind, she followed him back outside. His next stop became clearer the closer they got to the Black sisters, and Hermione hung back as he approached them on his own. Standing at attention, he offered the fruit to his grandmother. The tangerine looked as ripe as his nerves, but he didn't run. Narcissa didn't move to accept it, but she wore a soft smile Hermione had never seen directed at Scorpius before. She glanced at Hermione, who prompted her with a nod. She could do this. After another painfully awkward moment, Hermione almost abandoned her plan to not step in when NarcissaÑin probably the least graceful move she'd ever madeÑbeckoned him closer. Stiffly, Scorpius obeyed. Andromeda said something Scorpius didn't react to because he was staring at his grandmother. But when she brushed the hair from his forehead, he didn't flinch or move away, he just blinked at her in bewilderment. It was such a change from how she normally handled him. And change always got his attention. Narcissa's following words seemed as soft as her expression and his reaction confirmed it. Scorpius physically relaxed. Loosened his stance. Took a tiny, awkward step forward. Narcissa seemed to jolt, fumbling while peeling the fruitÑfrom her illness or her nerves, Hermione couldn't tell. Intently focused, he watched each of Narcissa's actions eagerly. Scorpius was slow to accept the offered tangerine slice, but he did so with a cautious smile, and only after dusting his little hands on his trousersÑsomething he never would have done, much less in front of his grandmother, when Hermione had first met him. His dimpled smile was hesitant yet present, and Narcissa returned one equally so. They bit down at the same time. It was a stunning sight. Thought-provoking and evocative. And when Scorpius backed away, scrambling back towards Hermione, Narcissa and Andromeda exchanged looks then smiles. Hermione barely had time to squat before he walked into her embrace, tucking his head into the crook of her neck, and letting go of the nerves he'd had all along. Narcissa and Andromeda looked over, catching her as she placed a hand on the back of his head. "You did so well, love." Scorpius lifted up, still flushed but smiling. He looked so proud of himself. "Ready?" He nodded. Scorpius wanted to help in the garden, but he certainly wasn't dressed for it. A quick trip into the house and she found Al's clothes for him to wear. Not long after, he was ready to work. With gloves of his own on, Scorpius followed Hermione, holding the empty wicker basket that was almost as big as him. She had picked from the plants outside, now it was time for the ones in the greenhouse. When she opened the greenhouse door for Scorpius, he only made it a few steps inside before he sat the basket down and tilted his head up. Now that he wasn't on a mission, Hermione found herself trailing after him as he freely explored every cornerÑplants, flowers, and herbs Ñtouching nothing, but looking around at everything that had grown since his last visit. Hermione peeled off his gloves. "Follow me." And he did, looking up at her more than the plants, which she only knew because she was looking back at him. She led him to the corner where the fruit she'd grown was plentiful and he pointed at the strawberry bushes, signing for permission to touch. "Of course." Hermione spoke and signed her permission at the same time. "I'll show you. Look for the bright red berries." Scorpius found one, and while there were many surrounding it, it was the first one he saw, the first one he cupped in his hand. Always so careful. So gentle. So respectful. "Is this the one?" He nodded with a small smile. She picked a strawberry next to it to show him how. "Watch me, okay?" He shuffled just a bit closer. "Grab the stem right here between your finger and thumb." She reached over and wiggled both which made him laugh. "Then pull and twist at the same time. Like this." Hermione did just that and the berry rolled into the palm of her hand. Scorpius' actions weren't quite as smooth but he was still successful. The smile he gave her was bright. Warm. Using her wand, Hermione rinsed off both strawberries and encouraged him to try, watching as Scorpius bit into the fruit. His eyes lit up at the sweetness before she bit into her own. From there, they tried the grapes and she laughed at the scrunched face he made when he tasted a fresh lemon. They picked more tangerines for him to take home, and Hermione went back and picked a few extra things to make for him at later points in the week. The basket was halfway full before Hermione insisted on carrying it, and though he handed it over, Scorpius kept his hand on the handle. Helping. The Arka plant was close to needing to be repotted; the once stubborn dittany was beginning to flourish, just like the boy at her side. When they finished in the greenhouse, there were still a few flower beds that needed weeding. She sent Scorpius to water the flowers along the edge of her fence with a watering can that was charmed to refill each time it emptied. Hermione snuck glances at Scorpius while she worked, watching as he stopped in front of each plant, stooped to its level, and touched the petals of each flower before watering it. Shaking her head, she continued cleaning out the soil while planning something to make for dinner. With Narcissa and Andromeda still on the blanket, there was no telling how long they would be there. Perhaps, if they stayed, they would dine around sunset and remain long enough for Scorpius to see the stars. Maybe they would all look. It was going to be a clear night. A perfect one for Draco to join them. To be present. A whisper on the wind caught her attention, and she turned to where Scorpius had moved on and was standing in front of the camellia that was barely taller than him. He craned his neck to see over the plant. But he couldn't. Hermione watched as he sat down the watering can and touched a flower. Just like he'd done before. Hermione moved on, but another sound drew her from her task again. A voice. Hermione slowly approached Scorpius, but he was too focused on the task of touching each flower with careful fingers before doing the same to the next. Almost as if he were greeting them. And then she heard it again. Soft and barely audible. Hoarse from lack of use but definitely his voice. She felt a rush of realisation and excitement, torn between approaching and retreating, but jolted when he picked up his watering can and moved along to the tulips, watering them and greeting each colourful flower with kindness and respect. Hermione stepped closer, focus zeroed in on the little boy who seemed to be in his own world. Her heart beat faster than a hummingbird's wings. Like most five-year-olds, he veered off task, catching sight of the roses on the other side of the vegetable patch. Off he went with the watering can in tow. From where he stood, Scorpius' back was to her completely. He sat the canister down, stooped low, and started from the bottom, touching the blooms and speaking one word to each. A word he had previously signed. Their first word. Over and over, he spoke to the plants. Hermione approached him from behind, placing a hand on his shoulder. It startled him a bit and he turned to her, eyes following each movement as she kneeled next to him. He returned her small smile before touching another bloom and leaning in to whisper a small greeting. Her hand trembled on his shoulder. "Are you saying hello to theÑ" Scorpius looked at her and only her, eyes bright and as blue as the sky. The only sign of his nerves was the hand that slipped into hers. Communication came in many forms. Sight, touch, and sound. And each form had taught Hermione its power. They started from looks and understanding and moved to signing with their hands. It was a journey of patience, hope, and love. Hermione found herself overwhelmed to the point of tears when Scorpius stepped closer. His face was brave and earnest as he finally spoke a single word. "Hi." The finest language is mostly made up of simple words. George Elliot Chapter End Notes A/N: Hope you enjoyed! Art for this chapter: DHr Cuddling by bookloverdream-blessedindeed & Big Spoon/Little Spoon pt 1 by bookloverdream-blessedindeed & Big Spoon/Little Spoon pt 2 by bookloverdream-blessedindeed & Hermione Painting by meriyart & Draco & Hermione Sleeping by Oukyn & Draco & Hermione Sketch by Camiiie4 Embedded art by Jaxx-in-a-box [ Instagram / Tumblr / Ao3 ] Chapter 28: ForgedinFire Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Twenty Eight Forged in Fire September 2, 2011 Baking was a combination of chemistry and art. Measuring, mixing, sieving, heating, and cooling. In this process, Hermione found control through calculated measurements, comfort in completing finite tasks, and a much needed sense of complete absorption that left no room for worry. Easy. Predictable. All she had to do was follow the instructions, remain calm, and at the end, there would not only be a sweet treat, but the chance of a clear mind. Today had left her with a need for that simplicity. A disagreement with Sachs over Andromeda's increased involvement in Narcissa's care, two moody MalfoysÑScorpius being the cuter and easier to consoleÑsecurity meetings at the hospital, and the Occupational Therapist's walkthrough of the Malfoy's home. Not to mention the rushed request to bottle the Wolfsbane Draco had just brewed for the four new wolves emerging from the last full moon. When Hermione finished her fifth batch of pastriesÑstrawberry crumb barsÑshe looked at the baking pans on every surface and began to wonder if there was a flaw in her theory. A roar of green flames erupted and out stepped Harry from her fireplace. "Your house smells like a cake shop." He joined her in the kitchen, a low whistle on his breath as he appraised her surroundings. "Looks like one, too. I should be terrified, but I don't see sad pies and I'm starving." Hermione smacked his hand before Harry could so much as think about reaching for a crumb bar. "Ow!" He looked exactly like James whenever he got caught misbehaving. "I wasn't going to Ñ" His smile grew. "Okay, I was. Can IÑ" "No, those are for the playdate tomorrow." "All of them?" Harry looked sceptical. "I thought you were stress baking." "I am." "About your parents?" His smile fell into a worried frown as he ran a hand through his messy hair. "Ginny told me. It explains why you looked on edge when you came to the restoration meeting. Did Percy call you?" "No." Hermione tensed with the memory of the day fresh in her mind. It hadn't quite left. She'd spoken to her father twice, short conversations, where they each kept trying to reassure the other that they were fine, but Hermione left both calls with the impression her parents weren't on speaking terms. The thought rattled her more than expected. "Then why did you come?" Realisation of her mistake dawned in that moment. The delay was a symptom of the stress that occupied too much of her mind. Harry rested his elbow on the island, waiting patiently for her excuse while Hermione reoriented and rapidly decided that he had to know something. Draco had thrown him out along with everyone else, and he'd observed them in the joke shop, at lunch, and on the walk to the forest. That didn't even include whatever he had noticed outside of her presence. However, what conclusion Harry had drawn for himself, she still didn't know. It was worth investigating. "You didn't answer my question." "Why do you think I came?" His simple, leading question was asked while she offered a lemon macaron. He devoured the treat in a single bite and held his hand out in a silent request for another. Which she allowed. "Answering a question with a question is what someone does when they're trying to stall for time." Harry laughed. "You're confirming my suspicions." "You're delaying." "You're trying to distract me with sweets." "Is it working?" "No. Besides, I've been meaning to ask for a while, butÉ" She knew the end of his sentence: things had been chaotic at best. "We could go around in circles or you could tell me the truth about what's going on between you and Malfoy." Harry held up a hand before Hermione could respond. "And don't say it's nothing. I've been wondering about this since you turned up during training to talk to himÑ the first time." Back after their fight. "Things between Draco and I areÉ hmm." "Can't say it out loud?" "He's courting me." Hermione's nerves were bad enough without noting just how quickly Harry's jaw dropped. She started to bite her fingernails, but stopped herself by bringing her fists to her sides. "We're, um. We're attending Pansy's party together and it won't be our first date." If possible, his eyes grew wider, more incredulous; his mouth opened and closed several times as he tried to form words without success. She looked away, down, and all around before settling on the strawberry crumb bars. There was no other way to soften the news, and, honestly, she should have expected the shock, but he had asked. "UhÉ" Harry finally cleared his throat. "Since when?" "July, but it'sÉ IÉ hmm." "I'm so confused. I thought since the joke shop, for certain." "No." She folded her arms. "But it's new and I genuinely have no idea what I'm doing." Hermione lagged in everything while Draco remained two steps ahead; she was flustered by each new suggestion and move he made. It was incredibly frustrating. The appearance of the small terrarium in Draco's office after she'd jokingly bought him a nerve plant that was as temperamental as him. The thirty minutes he waited while she agonised over picking a bottle of wine to share. The walk they took with Scorpius before dinner Wednesday, his little hands in theirs. The subtle attempt he made to gauge her birthday plans. "Do you fancy him?" Harry sounded pained to even ask. "IÉ" "No judgment if you do. I only ask because you seem jumpy." Hermione glanced at her watch. "We'll be late for the meeting." "It's literally one word: yes or no. Simple as that." Complexities aside, Harry was right. To its core, the answer was simple, confirmed during the oddest of moments. It was in the changes he'd made, and the inspiring way he constantly worked to better himself by allowing old wounds to heal. The morning tea and the few occasions he begrudgingly allowed her to answer one of his crosswordsÑwith his offered pen. The quiet formation of a Wednesday dinner tradition and the continuing trend of taking bits of time together. Draco consistently signed tomorrow to Scorpius and he and Narcissa were awkwardly trying to mend their bridges. Even outside of those moments, she found the answer in something as simple as the way he looked at her. "Yes. I do, butÑ" "Then that's all that matters for now." Harry rested a hand on her shoulder. "You're allowed to enjoy yourself." "I'm trying to." She was good at doing that when Draco was around. When he wasn'tÑwell, her brain went into overdrive at every opportunity. "I think he's so far ahead because he has to outthink me, and he's probably hesitant because I am. I have my reasons, we both do, and the list is long. It's hard to be impulsive with consequences weighing on my mind." "The consequences will be there regardless. I say live in the moment. Just for a moment." A chuckle escaped her. "I expected more of an argument against him." "I wouldn't stop you from finding what you're looking for, even if it means there's a chance Malfoy is it." Harry shrugged and offered his arm, which Hermione accepted, blinking wildly at her best friend. "Looks like I'm capable of change, too." The heated debate died when the door swung open and Kingsley appeared, blanketing the room in shocked silence. Not of astounded surprise, but of wonderment. Without being prompted, every person rose to their feet to offer him the respect he had earned equally through war and merit, but Kingsley's eyes scanned each face before gesturing for them to take their seats. He said nothing and remained standing until the last person settled in their chair. All eyes were on him. "I heard raised voices and assumed I was in the right place." Kingsley clasped his hands together, maintaining his signature poise and sharp gaze. "Apologies for my tardiness. I had a matter to tend to concerning my bees." He was wearing his blue beekeeping robes, but Kingsley still looked every bit the leader he had once beenÑthe one he still was. Hermione remained in awe of the attention he commanded with so few words, the respect he summoned from each of them, and the power he wielded without effort. Not to subjugate. To lead. "I am unaccustomed to the procession of these meetings." Kingsley sat next to Percy and left the seat at the head of the table empty. "Please continue." After a few moments of silence, the meeting resumed. Except now the goal was to impress the newest person in the room. "Tiberius is making reckless moves that are slowing down the process of government," said the Head of the Central Department. A completely different topic from before. "He cannot sack anyone in his office, as he has no valid reason to do so, but he can and has reduced their roles to nothing." Percy looked interested in the news. "Who has he given the power to?" "Trusted allies with no experience. His nephew acts as his assistant, reviews documents, and has started attending all of his private meetings." She caught Draco's eye from across the table, noting the way he stopped tapping his fingers and straightened in his seat. They were thinking similar thoughts. Cormac? With power? Tiberius had to be either desperate or fully insane. Neither were good options. Both were a recipe for disaster. "He's closing ranks faster than anticipated." Hestia looked a bit disturbed. "He knows the end is near." "Which is strange." Percy exchanged a glance with Hermione, who couldn't keep the flabbergasted look off her face. "The evidence we have is only circumstantial. Nothing is concrete yet, despite the unfinished searches. Nor are his actions illegal, as there is no precedent for the ethical codes he continues to violate. Outside of the firestorm caused by Skeeter's article and his approval ratings continually plunging, I feel like we're missing something vital." "Or Tiberius knows something we don't." Hermione folded her arms. "I'm not sure what it would be or if we could even find out." "Perhaps you might speak with CormacÑ" "Out of the question." She glared at the Head Ministry Librarian. "I am not bait." "Fine." The Head Ministry Librarian turned her attention to Draco. "And where are you with the translations, Mr Malfoy?" Hermione was instantly reminded of a previous discussion of this subject. The same they'd had right in this very room. When he adjusted his glasses and fixed his gaze on the older witch, she knew his response was going to be reminiscent of their last talk. Next to her, Harry shifted in his seat. Percy's eyebrow raised. "Hardly any time has passed since the last time this question was asked." "And? We would still like a response." A cord of tension stretched the expanse of the room. "Very well." Draco leaned back in his chair. "I have not completed any additional translation work." No one was happy. "I'm certain you are aware of the speed in which these translations need to be completedÑ" "I am, just as I am certain you are aware of the raid on the hideout near Ballycastle." Everyone was aware, even the public. "What the media doesn't know is that Death Eaters remain in the area, attacking the Aurors and Task Force members on site." Harry clenched his jaw. "Instead of translations, Malfoy has been performing his actual duties. Apologies. If any of you would like to assist us in bearing the responsibility of protecting the safety of dozens of people, please be my guest." Kingsley only observed, folding his hands on the table. She almost forgot he was there. The librarian flushed, chastened. "I was not aware of the extentÑ" "Nor should you be. We have a protocol that prevents us from divulging information." Posturing for Kingsley's sake continued; most stayed quiet while the usual suspects remained out front leading the charge. "Now that Kingsley is on board, and with Mr Malfoy currently indisposed, we should seek out another person to translate. Someone whoÑ" "Perhaps the break in translation will allow my team to review everything to date." Percy leaned forward, staring down the Head of the Department of Mysteries, who seemed ready for tonight's argument. "We do have some promising leads." Hermione glanced at Percy, intrigued not by his wording but by his tone. The air was buzzing with something more than friction. "If there are promising leads we don't need Mr Malfoy's efforts orÑ" "I assure you that you are incorrect." Kingsley's deep voice seemed to stun the room. "Regardless of Mr Malfoy's translation work, his involvement in this movement is vital." "Apologies, Sir." The librarian had an unforgettable flush across her cheeks. "But Mr Malfoy Ñ" "Is part of the reason I sit here today." The entire room took a collective breath. Everyone's attention was split between Kingsley's words and Draco, whose jaw was clenched so tight she wanted to reach over and smooth it, but he sat too far away. He only began to relax as the silence stretched on. And on. "The success of this movement has nothing to do with dismantling the current regime but rather in each action we make going forward." When Kingsley spoke, everyone took notice. "If we don't learn, we are destined to become those we seek to unseat." Kingsley looked around at each person, making certain he had their undivided attention. "The past is more than a set of events with an inevitable outcome. It is a text we can use for interpretation, a reminder that we can change the present, and thus, the future. The only way to learn from the past is to understand how the world we inhabit came into being." Tiberius. The Death Eaters. The corruption. The discrimination. The chaos. "We must take that understanding and use it so we do not continue to make the same mistakes or uphold the same prejudices as those before us. We have to be better, and by doing so, we become part of the solution instead of the problem." Looks were exchanged and people rustled around in their seats. "I am told my desire for fundamental social change is idealistic, that I should be pragmatic and focus on achievable goals such as ridding the Ministry of corruption. But what good is an orderly Ministry when we carry the past like a weight? We leave ourselves open to repeat it, just as we did with the second war." Hermione nodded. This was what they needed to hear. This was what they needed to understand. This was about more than power. This was about the future. "Mr Malfoy's presence is proof of the change I seek." Kingsley placed his hands on the arms of the chair. "He has a past we are all familiar with, a past he was exonerated from, but he represents the potential of what we can become as a society if we build bridges instead of burning them, if we treat people like people and not measure them against their family or history." Kingsley surveyed the room while Hermione kept her eyes trained on DracoÑnot that she could read him when he was staring so hard at the table. "Let us not forget we are all on the same side, and we fight the same fight, no matter the reason. You want to rid the Ministry of corruption? First you must uncorrupt yourselves. Starting here. Tonight." Draco raised his head. "Now that we've cleared that up, please continue." Percy did. The meeting ended not long after. Hermione filed out along with everyone else, leaving Kingsley to speak with Percy in private. Harry gave her a parting smirk with Draco at his side. They disappeared for several minutes. Lingering as close to the entrance of the library as allowed, Hermione waited patiently until Draco re-entered with her bag and offered it to her. "Thank you." Hermione draped the strap around her. "Are you finished or do you need to go back?" "I'm finished, but Kingsley is still speaking with Percy. They might be a while. Ready?" The smile she flashed spoke of her eagerness. Draco rolled his eyes, scoffing under his breath even as he took her hand and led the way. Tonight, Hermione perused while he retrieved the books left behind by the staff per his request. Hopefully, they would find something to aid his efforts in fusing an anti-Apparition charm to his mother's necklace. She cast a glance at him as he looked at the cover of each book then found her way to the Herbology section, picking up a few extra texts for some light reading before joining Draco. He did a double take when she approached. "I thought you were only selecting a few." "I did." Hermione placed all the books on the table. The sound echoed through the empty library. "This is a few." "Six, Granger?" Draco deadpanned. "Definitely more than a few." "I left three behind." A feat, really. One blond brow rose before Draco curled his finger, gesturing for her to come over. From her spot next to him, she peered at the books. It would take some trial and error to place an anti-Apparition charm on anything, but the research would help. "I can sort through these while you're gone and mark what might be useful." "Speaking of my absence." He turned to her fully. "I leave Sunday morning." The morning after the party. "How early? Scorpius will want to see you off." And, honestly, she did too, but she kept that to herself. "Potter and I have an on-site briefing at eight so after breakfast." Hermione nodded. "While I'm goneÑ" "I'll be around for Scorpius." Likely more than usual. "We'll have breakfast each morning and dinner on Wednesdays. We're reading a new book. And he and Albus have a playdate next Saturday at my house." Hermione leaned back against the table, eyeing Draco. "You're worried about leaving him?" "I am." "Don't." Hermione pushed off the table and started to move away but stopped when Draco's hand curled around her wrist. He was watching her, waiting for something she couldn't identify until his face hardened. A puff of exasperation escaped his lips before he let go. She tilted her head. "What is it?" "Nothing." September 3, 2011 The cactus was noticeably straighter. The recovery paralleled the little boy who stood next to it with his father's note in hand. Smartly dressed in a white shirt, dark blue trousers, and a green paisley bowtie, Scorpius looked all around before approaching where Hermione sat on the sofa. Once in front of her, she noticed that his bowtie wasn't a clip-onÑit was properly tied. Likely by Draco, who was lurking somewhere. Hermione rolled her eyes inwardly, hoping his odd mood from last night had passed, but she couldn't help but grin when Scorpius hugged her and murmured a small greeting in her ear. "Hi." "Hi there." Hermione would never get used to hearing his voice. Still little more than a whisper, it consistently made her smile. She pointed at the note still in his hand. "Are you reading to the cactus?" Scorpius nodded bashfully, shifting from foot to foot. Sensing his nerves, she offered a hand he accepted and his fidgeting stopped. "How do you know what it says?" The little boy leaned closer to her ear again and whispered, "Daddy told me." Hermione's eyes widened. Not only at his shy words but at his name for Draco. Burying his forehead into the crook of her neck, he sought the reassurance he always needed after speaking. It was nothing to swallow Scorpius into a hug, nothing to gently appreciate the fact that his single word responses had grown, all while trying to process the fact that Draco had begun reading his notes to Scorpius. How long had that been happening? "Did you like the note?" Scorpius nodded against her neck. Hermione didn't ask what it said. "Did your cactusÑ" "Mint." "Hmm?" She blinked. "Wait. The cactus is named Mint?" That time Scorpius lifted his head and nodded with that same timid smile. Hers grew wider. "I love it." Mint the cactus was the most adorable thing. It made her want to know his name for other things, but that would have to wait. "Are you ready to go see Albus?" Scorpius nodded with excitement. "Did youÉ tell your dad bye?" His blond head bobbed again as he slipped the note into his pocket, signing his affirmation a little slower. She understood the deeper meaning behind his return to silence. Scorpius had yet to speak to his father nor did he appear ready. She knew better than to hint at the idea. There was no rush. At least that was what Draco had said the night Scorpius spoke to her when he carried the sleeping boy inside after stargazing. "Did you say bye to Catherine?" Another nod. Hermione tapped her chin, pretending to think while his gap-toothed smile eagerly grew wider. Scorpius was adorable, and she couldn't stop grinning at his appearance, but he wasn't at all properly dressed for a playdate at the Burrow. Not that she could tell him that when he looked so proud of himselfÑand dapper. Hermione quietly hoped Ginny had spare clothes for when he inevitably ruined his. "I think we're ready to go then." When they stepped out the fireplace at the Burrow, Scorpius froze. His excitement drained into nervousness at the busy sitting room where Molly's knitting needles worked aggressively on two sweaters. The tentative boy at her side approached the moving needles, but he didn't let go of her hand. "It's magic." Hermione smiled when he turned his head in fascination. "Mrs Weasley makes jumpers for everyone for Christmas. Are you ready to meet her?" Scorpius signed his response and off they went, out of the sitting room and down the narrow hall. They moved much slower than usual; he was fascinated with every oddity and bit of clutter. This was entirely different from the clean lines of his own home, and even different from hers. When they made it to the kitchen, Molly greeted them both with a warm smile from her spot at the table. "Sorry we're late." "No, you're right on time." Then her smile lowered to the little boy. "You must be Scorpius. You look so handsome!" The nod he gave was accompanied by a blush as he fused himself to Hermione's side. Mrs Weasley, she could tell, was instantly enamoured, ready to fill him with good food and sweet treats. "Aw, you're such a dear. Albus is waiting foÑ" The door swung open and in rushed a floppy-haired Albus wearing jeans that were dirty at the knees and a shirt with cartoon characters. "Is ScorÑ" Then he saw his best friend, who instantly straightened and grinned at the sight of him. "You're here!" Albus practically tackled the blond boy with a hug that was eagerly returned. She was promptly showered with them by two boys. When they both pulled back, they stood side by side. They were the picture of oppositesÑAl's chaos to Scorpius' orderÑbut they were bonded by friendship. There were even bits about a promised story time from Molly that made Scorpius' eyes widen. It sounded like he was set to have a good day. "Where are James and Lily?" Hermione was sure Al would know. "Lily's asleep and James and grandad are poking some Muggle thing with a stick." That sounded about right. "Have fun." Hermione straightened Scorpius' bowtie. "I'll be back to get you a little later, okay?" A nod was all she received before Albus led a slightly bewildered but clearly happy Scorpius outside. The sound of Al's excitement could be heard long after the door shut behind him. "Draco Malfoy's son?" Molly was still smiling at her. "He's quite precious." It was interesting that Molly never once mentioned his family; not once did she say the words for a Malfoy. Scorpius was justÉ himself. A boy with a paisley bowtie who was playing with his best friend. It was moments like these when Hermione thought she couldn't possibly love Molly more. "Thank you." "What for, love?" "I justÑ" Hermione couldn't find the words. "If anything happens and I need to come back, just send a message and I'll come get him." Molly waved her off. "He'll be fine. Don't worry." Molly rested a hand on her shoulder. "Come, take a look." They peeked out the kitchen window to see Albus and Scorpius in the pasture with Arthur and James. It was sunny out, and they were smiling while picking wildflowers. All Hermione could think of was a time when she had wondered if he would like it here. Albus rushed to show his grandfather the flowers he picked and Scorpius slowly presented his own. "No, I had nothing to do with it." The forecast promised a pleasant, sunny day with a light breeze, but Hermione prepared for an impending storm nonetheless. Pansy. Hermione hadn't seen her since Girls' Night, but knew from Daphne's account of her spiralling that she wasn't doing well. Deep in pain and denial. Even Luna was trying to secure an early Portkey from Siberia. All attempts to convince her to cancel were met with blunt opposition so the most they could do was hope for the best and prepare for the worst. After a long shower to relax, Hermione took her time dressing, using a charm here and there to assist. Her dress was simple, understated, and lightweight. The pleasant shade of blue was so pale it looked white in a certain light, and slightly puffed sleeves accentuated the high-waisted bodice. Most importantly, it was comfortable enough to put on without issue. In fact, her only hesitation with the outfit was the cut of the neckline. Square and low, it was on par for the time period but it still made her modest heart skip a beat. Taming her hair with Sleakeazy's made forcing it into soft curls much easier. Pansy had been right to wait for a nice day, one not created by magic or wards. The sight from her vantage point at the top of the stairs was something out of a novel. Hermione forked a piece of fruit and ate it. "Who wants to go next?" "I'll go." Parvati opened her card. "Marriage in regency times versus now." She frowned. Today we're going to give all the workers biscuits to sayÑ" "Thank you?" Hermione nodded, that familiar swell of affection at hearing his quiet voice refused to be tamed. She straightened his bowtie. "Yes, we're going to say thank you. If you're ready to leave, just squeeze my hand and I'll bring you home." But there was a familiar, determined look in his eyes when he signed, Help. Like you. It grew. Those emotions. Her smile both widened and softened. "Okay, you can help like me." They started in the lobby, with Scorpius handing wrapped biscuits to the immediately enamoured Welcome Witch, who did everything except coo at him. Before Hermione could redirect him to the lift, the little boy had taken another package from the bag and handed it to an elderly man waiting by himself in the corner, who accepted the gift graciously. It snowballed from there, with Hermione watching after Scorpius, who was quite generously handing out toffee biscuits to everyone in the waiting area. The gesture was as sweet as the smile on his face was bashful; the tint of his cheeks turned rosy when thanked. Hermione was glad she'd made extras. "He has a good heart," the Welcome Witch said as they watched him hand biscuits to another child a few years older than himselfÑwho had a pair of antlers Scorpius didn't even blink at. "You should be proud to be his mum." "Oh, I'm not his mother." Scorpius began his short journey back to her side once his task was completed. "But I know she would be proud." Hermione's own words followed her through each stop they made, with blue eyes looking at her eagerly until she nodded her permission for him to hand out biscuits to every person they saw. Given the time of day, there weren't many, but the gesture was sweet. Watching a boy who shied away from people he knew give out biscuits to strangers made her sentiment for him swell. It wasn't about love or that Hermione did so fiercely, but rather about how she continued to learn facets of the word as it pertained to Scorpius. In different moments. At different times. No, he wasn't hers by blood, but six months of knowing and caring for him, watching him grow and learn, had changed her forever. She would go through the highs and lows all over again just to be right where she was: running out of biscuits while being pulled along by a little boy too generous for words. As predicted, they had none left when they reached Theo's office, but Scorpius had saved a biscuit for him, presenting it to his generally stoic godfather while standing at his side. "Thank you." Theo signed as he spoke and his smile grew when Scorpius signed, you're welcome back before approaching the bookshelf. He craned his head up to look at it fully as his mouth dropped open in awe. Hermione took her wand out to cast a feather-light charm on any book he picked. "Impromptu visit?" "Bringing biscuits for Employee Appreciation Week, though we ran out. Scorpius shared." The little boy grinned brightly. Not hiding despite the fact that Theo could easily look over his shoulder and see him. "He's quite proud of himself." "Draco told me he was taking a half day." "He is, but there was a Death Eater sighting this morning so I'm not certain if he'll get a chance." Theo went into his desk while Hermione cast the charm on the enormous book Scorpius was eyeing. Choice in hand, he settled on the chair next to her, feet adorably dangling as he opened a book on poisonous plants that she was glad he didn't fully understand. He must have been drawn to the colourful wild mushrooms on the page. Scorpius looked at her expectantly. A beat passed before she realised what he wanted. Digging into her beaded bag, she grabbed Scorpius the charmed earmuffs, which he put on happily. "Earmuffs?" Theo looked confused. "They block out sound and play music. I created them so he wouldn't be able to hear every conversation or get overwhelmed in large spaces. He likes them." At the perfect moment, Scorpius bobbed his head to whatever was playing and flipped to the next page. "Poisoning book?" "Forgive me for not having child-friendly literature in my office." They both laughed. "How have you been?" Hermione was genuinely curious. Neither of them had approached the topic of their last conversation in this very office. "Working through some things." It was more of an answer than she had expected. "Where are you and Draco on creating the potion?" "Testing the bases. We plan to do more with it now that he's back." Hermione crossed her legs. "Neville told me that the Silphium plant isn't budding yet, but that it might be too soon or it might be out of season so we're just going to be patient. Draco and IÑ" "Make quite the team." Theo's expression was a familiar neutrality. Hermione squinted. "This is a trap." "No, I'm merely stating the obvious: you're working with Narcissa's potion and care, as well as with the restoration efforts. Furthermore, you've been helping him with Scorpius, who is thriving. I hear Draco is putting him in school with Harry's son." "He is, but that's a decision he made on his own." Theo was poised to say more but there was a knock on his door. When he waved the door open everyone, save Scorpius, turned to the person standing in the doorway. Susan. "We have a situation." Twenty wizards. All males of differing ages with indistinguishable features. All found unconscious but alive in a park near Little Whinging after Aurors were called to the scene. A scene they were still investigating. None had identification and were cold to the touch, which was where the similarities stopped and the differences began. Different clothing and appearances. Hermione was more than willing to leave it to the Aurors when they arrived but there was one issue: None of them could be revived. Susan had tried everything before calling for TheoÑand by extension, Hermione. Theo stayed behind in his office with Scorpius, who she'd supplied with snacks and a juice box, along with his parchment to draw with Albus and some other books she'd forgotten she had. Theo's amusement grew with each item she'd laid on his desk, especially the wipes. For his hands. He didn't like to get them dirty and cleaning charms weren't enough. Truly couldn't be helped. Besides, Scorpius really was grouchy when he was hungry, and from how things were going, it might be a while. Not that Hermione knew that when she left him. By the time Hermione arrived in the hall where the bodies were lined in two rows, there were eight other Healers, including Padma, gathered together talking about the strange case but not coming up with any solutions. Which led to them dividing into teams of two, changing into coverall robes with Bubble-Head Charms, and taking each patient into a room. It was better than keeping them out in the open. Hermione paired up with Padma, and with a frown marring her face, she watched as each diagnostic charm resulted in identical readings for both patients. "Padma, I'mÑ" "Suspicious as hell?" Her voice sounded muffled from inside the Bubble-Head Charm. "Me too. OnlyÉ I don't know of what exactly." "Has anyone checked for the presence of poisons or stunning?" "No poisons registered on any magical testing. No evidence of stunning." "What about Muggle testing?" Padma's eyes lit up. "I hadn't thought about that." "I'm not sure where to start." Hermione scrambled to think. "We'd have to ask a Healer from Poisonings. It's been a long time since I've worked there, but they do more blood work than any other department and they have connections with Muggle labs and contract Toxicologists." "It's a good place to start." "Let me get Scorpius home and I'll come back to help." At Padma's raised eyebrow, Hermione smiled. "He came with me to hand out toffee biscuits for Employee Appreciation Week. " "Ah, so he's the little boy handing out biscuits." Padma smiled fondly. "A few of my patients came in with them. They were all in good moods today. I think that's why Group Therapy went so well. Hermione waited until she was in the hall to shed her protective gear and make the trip back to Theo's office, stopping quickly by the open door where Susan and her partner were talking while looking over their own parchment. She quickly stepped in to have a look at their results and found them to be identical. Obviously, whatever happened to one, happened to them all. "If they all wake suddenly, we might have confusion. We should call for security." Both Healers nodded and the other excused himself to make that happen. When he was gone, Susan turned to Hermione. "Do your two have the same light marks on their fingertips?" She showed them both. "Looks likeÑ" Familiar to the ones on Mrs Weasley. And Mathers. "They've all been poisoned, butÑ" It wasn't right. If they weren't poisoned or stunned in a way that could be magically detected, their identical results didn't make much sense. But if they were, and it was somehow hidden from the tests, it would be a little more plausible. But their fingers, the burn marksÑthat didn't add up. Walking into Theo's office before knocking afforded Hermione the rare opportunity to see the man himself in his true form. Hospital owner and current coloured marker holder. Chaos answered her question with an explosion strong enough to send her sprawling, but instead it put her into suspended animation, instinctively grabbing the startled Scorpius' chair to stop him from toppling over. What was light bled into darkness. Source: Jaxxinthebox Hermione floated. Drifted. Stepped into a fog somewhere in between. She allowed it to close around her. She knew to hold on and fight. She knew she should cling to fleeting awareness and pain and never let go. But a selfish part of her wanted to keep floating in the mist. She wanted to let the pull she felt take her away. But there was something that kept her there. Something. Something. Hermione opened her eyes mid-gasp as if resurfacing from being submerged too long. Her vision was blurred. Pain. Dulled like a lost memory, she felt only semi-attached to it. And her body. Hermione could tell it hurt, she could feel the rumble of shock in her tight chest, but she couldn't feel it like she should. It sounded like Theo, but Hermione couldn't be sure. "Hermione, can you hear me?" "Yes." Her voice sounded foreign to her own ears. Raspy and hoarse. From screaming. The memory jolted her and made her panic. She couldn't move or see. Every muscle pulled taut. Rising terror swelled in her chest. Hot. Suffocating. "I-I-I can't feelÑ" Anything. "Hermione, don't try to move." It sounded like Susan. Her voice was soft but she could hear the stress in it. The distension. "You're Splinched and you've lost a lot of blood. We're working to heal you." Susan inhaled and fell quiet. "Theo, it's notÑ" Whatever Susan was about to say was muffled. "I need you to remain calm." Easier said than done. Her vision began to clear, something that should have made things better, but had the opposite effect. The artificial lights burned. Hysteria crept up on all sides of her consciousness. Each new spasm made it harder to keep still. Hermione took two gasping breaths to force it down. It wasn't working. She needed to know. "HowÉ bad?" Hermione coughed, trying not to choke on the lump in her throat. It burned. "T-truth." There was a pause. "Even after the first dose of antidote, the poison isn't making healing you easy." Bad. Very bad. Okay, so maybe she didn't need to know. "H-howÉ did you findÉ me?" "We didn't. He did." Hermione felt something being pulled tight, hard to tell where, and wheezed. "Got it! Perfect." Susan sounded momentarily relieved. "Shite, your heart rate is too high. I need you to calm down." "I can't." Breathe. Panic and pain rose steadily as each sense returned. Her vision and hearing sharpened. "Oh, thank gods," Susan said suddenly. "Get over here!" Hermione had a moment to question before she felt something that wasn't pain. A hand in hers. Warm green eyes and the face of her best friend. Comfort. Family. Harry. He looked terrible. The moment their eyes met, she saw it. Fear. "Everything clear?" Theo sounded gruff and impatient. "I need to move her from the welcome area. Is it safe?" "Enough. Roger moved Padma into the last undamaged room. She's stable." Hermione would have sobbed if she could. "It'll have to work. I'll go make preparations." She heard Theo get to his feet, his shoes squeaking on the floor as he ran. "Harry, hold her arms while I levitate her. MalfoyÑ" Draco. Hermione had no idea he was even there and didn't lay eyes on him until they got her off the floor. A streak of red stained his chin. Drying blood painted his neck. A bloody piece of parchment stuck out of his front pocket. A note. Everything flooded back. Scorpius. "H-he's safe." Another cough burned from her chest. "I know." He looked away. "Bones, whatÑ" "Shut up, Malfoy. I'm busy putting her back together. Make Hermione relax." It was impossible when she felt herself fraying at the seams. Habit made her seek out Harry first, but the naked fear in his eyes reignited her nausea. It was slow but she turned back to Draco, keeping her focus trained on him. This was easier. She couldn't read himÑdidn't know what he was feeling or what was going through his head. His face was stone. The world narrowed further when she felt his warm hand press against her forehead. His other slipped into her hand when she coughed; her body shook with heaving breaths as she struggled for air. He squeezed tight enough for her to feel the slight tremble. "Susan." Harry's voice was faint. "I know. I'm doing everything I can." Pain rose like a symphony of agony, growing louder and more complex, deafening her to the sounds of everything except her own ragged, gargled breaths. A storm of emotions and fear raged inside her, drowning everything outÑexcept the peace in his eyes. Something calm Hermione clung to desperately, but no matter how hard she tried, it was no use. Shock began clouding her vision. Dulled. Greyed. Darkened. Faded. There was nothing except sound. A voice speaking one word. A plea. "Hermione." And in the middle of my chaos, there was you. Paullina Simons Chapter End Notes A/N: Welcome to Act Three of Measure of a Man. The arrival of the inevitable and the hardest scenes I've ever written. *Pants* Art for this chapter: Forehead Kisses & Goodbyes by meriyart & Hermione & Scorpius say Goodbye by ayaka-arts & Hermione Fighting by bookloverdream-blessedindeed & Pooltime Scorpius (Hopeful Gift) & Imagining of the end of Chapter 29 by ecaworks & DracoÕs POV of finding Hermione by Oukyn Embedded art by Jaxx-in-a-box [ Instagram / Tumblr / Ao3 ] Chapter 30: HoldingOn and Letting Go Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Thirty Holding On and Letting Go Falling. Simultaneously loud and deafeningly silent. Chaotic and peaceful. Painful and beautiful. Time passed through Hermione's fingers like smoke. Meaningless. Shapeless. Each failed swipe of her fingers spread her senses thinner. Lost in the centre of an endless maelstrom, darkness and light fought for dominance. They tugged and dragged her from one extreme to the next on a cosmic pendulum. Hermione took a breath but ice and fire were the only things entering her lungs. Landing was always the hardest part of falling. Her world was reduced to a roaring sea of pain. Desperate and hopeless. Drifting. Sinking. "G-go." A garbled whisper struggled from her lips. Pulse hammering, she reached blindlyÑ desperately. "He's scared." There were voices. Orders given. Fragments of sentences. Footsteps rushed all around her. She felt as though she were being stabbed repeatedly and weighed down. The air was heavy. But there was energy surrounding her that had no name. A voice in her ear. "I will." "Draco, hold her steady." Hermione burned in the light. Welcoming the heat, she landed in a field that stretched farther than her eyes could see. Blood bloomed into a sea of roses, wildflowers, and a garden full of fruit. The howl faded into the sigh of her name. Sobs morphed into a broken lullaby. Pain ebbed into heady pleasure while dust fell like benevolent rain from the sky. Tears transformed into a flowing river that created new paths and changed course before her eyes. It washed her clean. Healed her. Made her wholeÑfree. Hermione allowed herself to be carried away by the crimson and gold rays of light dancing around her. Laughing, she reached for the flowers as they harmonised a tune. She touched the silk petals as radiance cradled her, lifting her up into the air on a cloud of rainbows. Her gown shimmered and shifted colours in a dazzling display. Joy. Delirious. Euphoric. Real. But lonely. Flashes of objects beat in a rich hum of shades and shapes that moved in sync with her pulse. She felt each thump as if her heart reached from her head to her toes. Magic ran through her veins. Arms outstretched, water poured from her fingertips, giving life to the flowers beneath her feet. Hermione felt weightless. As if she were pure magic herself. "You're hallucinating." Unlike Icarus, the voice stopped her before she flew too close to the sun. A lily sprouted from the earth and its petals opened to reveal a vial. "Drink." Hermione did as she looked around for the source. Colours pulsed brighter and brighter like a beacon trying to grab her attention. And soon she was floating with the breeze between her fingers and fanning through hair as the cloud transformed to an effervescent rainbow. She wanted to touch the rainbow, and reached out, but it slipped through her fingers like mist. Like a dream. Or a nightmare. Hermione opened her eyes. She sucked in as much air as her lungs would allow and sputtered until whispered words cleared her clogged airway. The lights were harsh and cold. Hospital. Blue eyes. Red hair. Freckles. Flushed skin. Ron. He couldn't disguise his shock, anxiety, or relief. "How the bloody hell are you awake?" Hermione turned her head and retched. Gagged. Choked. Shook. She couldn't stop. "Susan!" The field hadn't lost its glow. Flowers swayed to the beat of her heart as the rainbow curled around her like a hug. Warm. Peaceful. The lilies fed her vials of sweet dew. The earth sprouted fruits. Rain fell from the sky, but Hermione remained dry as she picked roses that pricked her fingers then bloomed more. But something was odd. Each time she tried to focus, or began to wonder just where she was, the world became more vibrant. It worked hard to distract her from the rumble of thunder. Trouble. "Breathe." Hermione's pulse kicked with a sudden spike of fear that slipped alongside the euphoria and melted into the space in between. The light suddenly felt too hot and fear sharpened her senses until she could point out the inconsistencies. The floating. The colours. The petals. Nothing was right. She screamed into the technicolour void. "I didn't take anything! Please! I didn't take anything!" "You're not there." Molly's voice was faint in her ear. "She can't hurt you anymore. It's not real." But the pain was. Screaming and begging filled her ears. The voice sounded like her own, only hoarse and brokenÑweak from agony. She was drowning in a repeated Unforgivable on a drawing room floor. Everything blurred until nothing but white noise remained. Every muscle pulled taut like a wire. Gritting her teeth, Hermione convulsed to the maniacal cadence of laughter. Hermione napped under the warmth of the sun. "Drink" She did and everything was right. And wrong. Panic. It settled like the fog in the corner of her eyes. That hadn't been there before. Or had it? Colours became bolder, but Hermione focused on the fog, ignoring all that was pleasant. It wasn't real. This was her subconscious. A dream. "Dose in." Hermione gasped and the tightness in her chest unwound just enough to carve out one desperate, panting breath. Then another. Air was lava in her lungs. "She's burning through the cooling charmsÉ" "Slower breaths." Andromeda was out of sight but close. "Focus." Hermione followed the thread of her voice and the world sharpened to reveal Draco above her. His pale, drawn face. Grey eyes fixed on hers. He was normally warm but now his hands felt cold like relief. "Her heart rateÉ" Draco looked as though he'd crawled through the entirety of hell. "I'm still there." Hermione's fingers skimmed the top of the wildflowers when something changed. Pain. It invaded the field and attacked her from all sides. Seeped into her pores. Dug into every bone, muscle, and nerve. Pain was a shocking reminder of the loop she was stuck in. It was just a dream. A hallucination. The truth burned. Hermione writhed with the scent of her own flesh in the air until darkness rose. A vial of dew sprang forth from a lily as the voice took a shadowy corporeal form. "Drink." The word kick-started her memory. Each request. Each vial. She always accepted. Always trusted the voice and drank. It was merely a creation of her subconscious. It wouldn't hurt her. The shadow hovered next to her; its presence doused the flames and healed her charred skin, but it did little for the pain. When she drank, she relished in the relief until her chest heaved as she reeled from the residual agony. "It hurts. It burns." Hermione reached for the mist and came away with ash. "I know." "I'm so tired." The liquid cooled the fire within. "I want to go home." "It's almost over." The fog began to fade and Hermione's chest constricted as tears burned tracks down her cheeks. "Stay with me. Please." She sobbed so hard she thought she would shatter. "I'm tired of being alone." "You're not." The brilliance of the world faded. The spectre vanished and something dark and enormous rose from the depths. It wrapped its arms around her and dragged her into the darkness. "You're here." She wasn't surprised to see Draco in her dreams, but he faded like the flame of a covered candle. "This isn't a dream." Darkness. The cold void was calm like a stream and cool like a breeze. A peaceful and perfect silence so absolute and enduring it penetrated her soul. Hermione hovered above the current, highs and lows blurring together, and she wondered if this was what it felt like to fly. Or fall. Just when she thought the darkness would last forever, beams of light sliced through. They reached in the void and pulled her out. A sudden bang rang out, the sky returned to clear, and all the visions disappeared. She was at the beginning again. And time rolled on. Light. Consciousness crept through her addled mind. Dizzy and groggy. Memories flooded in a surge that froze the breath in her lungs. Hermione's vision sharpened. She heard herself groan as she made out features. A face. "Oh fuck." Pansy. Hermione returned to the field. Darkness and light cycled, but now she laid in the sun; it was warm on her face and body and hands. The shadow returned to her lonely world for seconds before leaving. And each time the pain of its loss tore at the fabric of a reality that didn't exist. Darkness. The next face she saw was bathed in ribbons of sunlight filtering through the window. Luna. "Where am I?" Hermione rasped out. "Shhh." A cool cloth touched her head. "Dreams fly on the wings of time." She didn't understand but her questions were hushed and soothed away. "Back to sleep you go." Hermione helplessly obeyed. Reality shocked Hermione with a punch of adrenaline that should have jolted her into an upright position, but she couldn't feel her arms or legs, let alone move. She was only capable of blinking and breathing. Needing to calm the rising panic, Hermione focused her efforts on the simple act of wiggling her toes. The relief that accompanied the sensation was palpable. "Kingsley." She could pinpoint Pansy's voice from anywhere. "Theo. Wake up!" Wincing at the sheer volume, Hermione tried and failed to turn away as if she were the too-bright sun. "It's too soon for her to be awake." What? There was a sudden noise to her left before a dishevelled Theo entered her sight line, a picture of unkempt hair and weary eyes. And then there was Kingsley, who looked poised as always, slightly haggard yet relieved. Neither spoke at first. They exchanged looks then turned to Pansy as if she held all the answers. Hermione was beyond confused. More so when Theo ran a hand through his hair and swore. "Can we hide this?" Kingsley looked deadly serious. "She's not prepared." "It's the middle of the night. We can't smuggle her out now." Pansy bit down on her fingernail. "The plan was to take her toÑ" "Not an option in her current state." Theo shook his head. "At the very least, I need twelve hours before enough of the potions are out of her system to safely transfer her." Hermione wiggled her toes to get someone's attention. It wasn't enough. "The atrophyÑ" "Snap out of healing mode." Pansy sounded harsher than Hermione had ever heard her speak to Theo. "She cannot be here when anyone realises she's awake. The media is a bloody circus downstairs. Andromeda and Percy keep catching Ministry officials and reporters trying to gain access to this roomÑoh, and let's not forget about our biggest problem: the bastard." "Colourful language aside, Miss Parkinson is correct." Kingsley spoke her name with a sort of familiarity that sparked Hermione's curiosity. She knew for a fact the two had never met. "You and Susan have a conference to update the media on her condition in the afternoon. Is that enough time?" "Tiberius will be around for an inspection before then." Hermione blinked rapidly, but no one noticed. "She can fake it." What? "I'll make certain Percy is here with you and Draco during the inspection. Keep it quick. Mind Draco's temperÉ" Hermione had never been more confused, not just by their words, which quickly devolved into a rapid fire three-way debate, but she couldn't wrap her mind around anything enough to keep up with the conversation. Her gaze swept her surroundings. Situated on the edge of her periphery was a colourful display with multiple vases of elaborate flower arrangements. They were beautiful, but her eyes caught on something smaller. Insignificant to some, but it meant the world to her. A small cactus. Mint. It was good that she couldn't speak. Her heart was in her throat. Sudden sensation roused her once more; bleeding through her veins, it brought her limbs back to life. Hermione still struggled to move, but she managed to open and close one of her hands, wiggle the fingers of her other, and flex the toes on both feet. She wanted to turn back to where the cactus should have been, but she was blinded by a penlight. "Welcome back again." Theo shone the light in her other eye. "How do you feel?" "I-I don't know," Hermione managed, voice raw and scratchy. He reached out of sight and brought back a cup with a straw. She took a greedy sip, the water tasted better than anything, but he didn't let her gulp. "What happened?" "Too much. I'd rather not overwhelm you." He looked over his shoulder. "Go getÑ" "Already gone." Pansy's footsteps echoed across the floor before the door opened then shut while Theo ran diagnostic charms and Kingsley raised her bed. Theo seemed pleased with the results of the scan, but Hermione couldn't stop staring around the room. There were plants and flowers on every surface. But her focus went back to the cactus. "Looks like the poison is out of your system." "Can youÑ" Hermione coughed and her chest ached with phantom pain. "Tell me the truth." "Okay." Kingsley took Theo's seat when he moved; patience radiated off him in a way that calmed her. "How long has it been?" Theo looked supremely uncomfortable. "A week." Hermione tried to absorb the gut punch of the news. Seven days. Last she remembered, it was the fifteenth, which meant it was now the twenty-second, but she had no concept of the hour. Only that it was dark. Not that it mattered. Once again, time had passed without her knowing, without her having any memory of it. Blinking up at the ceiling, she took several deep breaths to calm herself down. At least she tried to. Kingsley held her hand, but said nothing when she looked away to swallow the growing lump in her throat. She had missed her birthday. As petty as it seemed, it was all she could think about and obsess over until the thought left her weary enough to know she had to let it go. There was nothing to do about it. Nothing that would change the past. There were more pressing matters and questions that needed answers. "My injuries?" "Blunt?" "Please." Theo still had the parchment in hand but didn't look at it. He didn't need to. Multiple fractures of her ribs and wrist, Splinching, damaged lungs, a concussion with a head wound, and an abdominal laceration. Some injuries she remembered, others she didn't. "The Splinching and poison were the largest concerns. The poison had been in your system far too long and we had difficulty treating you because of this, but Draco had the idea of mixing the antidote with Replenishing Potion to force it out faster and it worked." Theo gave her a moment to absorb his words. "Your body is under a lot of stress and you're on an obscene amount of potions right now. We had to get creative with the combination." "I don't feel much of anything. Heavy. Groggy. It's hard to think. I feel like I could sleep for years but I know I just woke up. A little pain butÑ" "If you're able to feel any pain I'm shocked, but we've begun the process of weaning you off the heavier pain potions. We can manage it carefully and keep you as comfortable as possible." For the first time, Hermione paid attention to the state of her arm when Theo reached across her. Wrapped in heavy bandages, she could barely feel her fingers. "How bad is it?" "The trauma to your wrist was extensive. The rest of your arm was partially Splinched so the bandage is to protect your new skin." Theo touched the tips of each finger. "We did as much as we could, fixed the ligaments, regrew skin and the bones, but you'll need therapy and time to heal." "Will I fully recover?" Brewing. Cooking. Working. And so many other ways she used her hands. This was a nightmare. "Right now I'm not certain as it's not my area of expertise. Susan thinks there's a high chance you'll end up with a tremor and believes you should start therapy as soon as possible. Nothing has been done on that front because we wanted to see how you were when you woke up. You had two seizures in all of this andÑ" "Okay." Hermione felt that news like a brisk wind. Stunned, she shivered while struggling to process everything, but there was more and her curiosity wouldn't allow it to be left unsaid. "Keep going." "The seizures were a result of the poison, not stress." That didn't make it any better. "You mentioned Splinching, I justÑwhere?" "Down your right side. You lost skin, muscles, hair, a few fingers, and half your leg in the process. We reattached and regrew what was lost, butÉ the process wasn't simple because of how it was Splinched." He moved back the covers and she saw her legÉ as well as the ugly sight of bruised and raised skin that stretched from her knee to her foot. A straight line. Half her leg in the most literal of terms. Hermione couldn't fathom the sight. No wonderÑ Kingsley covered it up. "What else?" She swallowed thickly. Theo's face shifted to concern. "Let me know ifÑ" "Just tell me." Theo and Kingsley had a silent conversation that ended with the latter nodding before Theo continued. "We grew your skin and muscles back, and used healing charms and as much dittany as we could to close the wounds. But there will likely be scarring on your arm and down your right side. Roger had to stitch you up to hold you together long enough for the antidote to begin working before we were able to start repairing the damage. The poisonÑ" "From the boxes?" "Yes." Kingsley nodded after Theo gestured for him to take over. "It was packed into boxes that were planted and timed to go off and cause maximum chaos while the Aurors were dealing with a different situation in the city." "One didn't work though, right? I rememberÉ" The last one. "You're correct. It didn't take Harry long to figure out how they made it past security and he apprehended the sympathiser who confessed straightaway. She's cooperating, by the way." "Who?" "A Mediwitch that was coincidentally injured in the initial blast. She said you revived her, healed her, and told her to hide. Your compassion moved her. It made her talkÉ" Hermione's mind was racing. "PadmaÑ" "Released three days ago." "I want to see her." "We need to talk about that, but it's also the middle of the night and we have to figure it outÉ" The rest of his words were lost. Memories flashed wildly in her mind. Words. Pain. Healing Padma. Falling leaves. A howl. Crashing. A room with faces. So much blood. There was a hand on her shoulder and another one squeezing hers. Kingsley. "Your heart rate just spiked." Theo's concern was evident despite the calm he tried to project. "We don't have to talk about it." "But we do." Kingsley frowned slightly. "Much has happened since and she needs to be informed of the basics at the very least." "Like what?" "The public outcry after the attack on St Mungo's has put the Ministry in a position where they must take decisive action.
